task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: 3D path for the wire is created
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D sketch, Draft wire, or set of lines representing the 3-segment path (A to B, B to C, C to D) is visible in the 3D viewport or the model tree view.

task_completion.description: 20 mm radius fillets are applied to the intermediate corners
task_completion.visual_evidence: The sharp intermediate corners of the path are rounded off. A fillet operation or rounded sketch corners should be visible in the 3D view or model tree.
A circular sketch or profile (representing the 6 mm diameter) is visible, typically placed at the start of the wire path.

task_completion.description: Single solid 3D wire model is generated
task_completion.visual_evidence: A solid 3D tube/wire object following the bent path is visible in the 3D viewport. The model tree should show a Sweep, Pipe, or AdditivePipe feature.

task_completion.description: Document is saved with the correct filename
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD window title bar, active document tab, or a save dialog shows the filename 'seat_frame_wire.FCStd'.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the FreeCAD GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the FreeCAD interface with toolbars, model tree, and 3D viewport being actively used, rather than just terminal commands.

integrity.description: Model was created using FreeCAD tools, not imported as a finished file
integrity.visual_evidence: The model tree (Combo View) shows the construction history (e.g., Sketches, Drafts, Fillets, Sweeps) rather than a single imported solid or mesh.

integrity.description: The result is a genuine 3D model
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport displays a rendered 3D object with shading and perspective, not a 2D drawing or text representation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected filename is 'seat_frame_wire.FCStd'. The final model must be a solid bent wire with a bounding box of approximately 106x106x56 mm (this accounts for the 6mm diameter wire extending 3mm past the 100x100x50 mm geometric path in all directions). The expected volume is roughly 8330.6 mm³. The path must feature 20mm radius fillets at the two intermediate corners.
